elements.title: reign of Augustus
elements.contents: ... that the reign of Augustus (bust pictured) relied on chance, experimentation and improvisation, according to one historian?

elements.title: Walla Walla River
elements.contents: ... that in 1936 the Supreme Court ruled that Oregon could divert all of its own water in the Walla Walla River from Washington?

elements.title: Sam Jaffe
elements.contents: ... that Sam Jaffe brought "wine, cheese and live caterpillars" to gallery openings of his photographs?

elements.title: Big Mama
elements.contents: ... that Flying Lotus created Big Mama from 10 to 15 seconds of music produced each day over two months?

elements.title: a 2009 series of missile strikes by the US
elements.contents: ... that Yemen originally took responsibility for a 2009 series of missile strikes by the US?

elements.title: a bridge in Ontario
elements.contents: ... that a bridge in Ontario was named after an Ojibwe word meaning 'dawn' or 'morning light'?

elements.title: Habiburrahman El Shirazy
elements.contents: ... that the debut novel of Habiburrahman El Shirazy beat out Harry Potter as Indonesia's "favourite book" of 2005?

elements.title: New Protection
elements.contents: ... that Australia's New Protection policy made tariff relief for manufacturers contingent on paying employees a living wage?

elements.title: Gao Cai
elements.contents: ... that Gao Cai, a Chinese eunuch during the Ming dynasty, was accused of consuming the brains of young boys in the belief that it would restore his penis?
